title: Shared-gating predicted variance-floor repair on frozen widened bridge surface

paper_facing_comparison.note: This is the paper-facing comparison surface reported by NVRC.

local_smoke_reference.note: This is a bounded current-machine feasibility surface and is not paper-comparable to UVG.

metrics.label: uvg_bd_rate_reduction_pct_vs_vtm_ra_psnr
metrics.description: Paper-facing headline coding-gain metric reported by NVRC on UVG under the random-access PSNR setting.
metrics.derivation: Taken from the NVRC abstract as the reported average coding gain over VVC VTM (RA) on UVG measured in PSNR.

metrics.label: local_resnet18_teacher_smoke_loss_value
metrics.description: Current-machine feasibility smoke metric for the repaired real-teacher path.
metrics.derivation: Read directly from the bounded resnet18_imagenet smoke run after wiring the teacher branch into the local NVRC tree.

metrics.label: tiny_local_teacher_pilot_bpp_avg
metrics.description: Average bits-per-pixel from the bounded tiny-local main-entry pilot with the repaired teacher path.
metrics.derivation: Parsed from the completed tiny-local pilot result summary produced by main_nvrc.py after training, encoding, decoding, and re-evaluation.

metrics.label: tiny_local_teacher_pilot_psnr_avg
metrics.description: Average reconstruction PSNR from the bounded tiny-local main-entry pilot.
metrics.derivation: Parsed from the completed tiny-local pilot result summary emitted by the repaired local NVRC main entry.

metrics.label: tiny_local_teacher_pilot_teacher_mse_avg
metrics.description: Average teacher-consistency MSE from the bounded tiny-local main-entry pilot.
metrics.derivation: Parsed from the completed tiny-local pilot result summary after the repaired teacher loss path was exercised end to end.

metrics.label: beauty_teacher_smoke_bpp_avg
metrics.description: Historical local teacher-enabled Beauty smoke average bits-per-pixel preserved from the exported quest state.
metrics.derivation: Recovered from the prior exported run snapshot for the completed Beauty teacher tiny smoke retry3 record.

metrics.label: beauty_teacher_smoke_psnr_avg
metrics.description: Historical local teacher-enabled Beauty smoke average PSNR preserved from the exported quest state.
metrics.derivation: Recovered from the prior exported run snapshot for the completed Beauty teacher tiny smoke retry3 record.

metrics.label: beauty_teacher_smoke_teacher_mse_avg
metrics.description: Historical local teacher-enabled Beauty smoke average teacher-consistency MSE preserved from the exported quest state.
metrics.derivation: Recovered from the prior exported run snapshot for the completed Beauty teacher tiny smoke retry3 record.

metrics.label: beauty_no_teacher_partial_teacher_mse
metrics.description: Historical partial no-teacher comparator teacher-MSE checkpoint kept only as contextual reference.
metrics.derivation: Recovered from the first recorded finite checkpoint at step 144/576 in the exported no-teacher Beauty smoke snapshot.

metric_contract.comparability_note: Preserves the canonical baseline metrics while adding bounded-bridge local metrics for this run-specific comparison surface.

run_context.dataset_scope: bounded local bridge subset ego4d_small_bridge_16f with 16 frames at 32x32 resolution; same frozen widened bridge surface used for the relation-repair comparison.
notes: Treat this as the first valid bounded evidence point for the predicted-side variance-floor line, not as a paper-facing win.
The archived first attempt remains useful only as debugging evidence because the configured regularizer never became active there.
Canonical baseline metrics are carried through in the record to preserve the accepted comparison contract; the run-specific bounded-bridge metrics are the newly measured evidence for this branch.

hypothesis: A minimal predicted-feature variance-floor regularizer on the same frozen widened bridge surface can improve bounded teacher-consistency without changing the external packet export/evaluation surface.
setup: Dedicated run branch/worktree on the active idea line. Resume root: shared_gating_interface_export_ego4d16f_chunk00_smoke_r1. Task config keeps semantic-change weighting and temporal-delta consistency enabled, turns on predicted-feature variance floor with weight=1.0 and margin=0.1, and keeps predicted-delta variance floor off. The external packet export/eval surface stays fixed to the same bounded ego4d_small_bridge_16f bridge subset.
execution: First bounded run on this branch exposed a real plumbing bug: the new variance-floor fields were present in args.yaml but were not forwarded through create_overfit_task(...), so the effective pred_variance_weight stayed at 0.0 in both train and eval logs. That invalid first output was archived, the missing passthrough was fixed in tasks.py, syntax was rechecked, and the corrected bounded run was relaunched and completed successfully under bash session bash-9cf7c4a3.
conclusion: The minimal predicted-side variance-floor repair is now validated as an effective live regularizer in the real bounded main-run path, but its first measured trade-off is mixed rather than clearly positive. It is good enough to support route judgment, but not good enough to claim that this repair family already improves the bridge end to end.

progress_eval.reason: No verified breakthrough over the active baseline or previous best.

delivery_policy.reason: Research paper mode is enabled, but the current run does not beat the baseline clearly enough. Revise the direction or strengthen the method before writing.

objectives: find a link between the current comression layer and build the large model layers to make the whole pipelien runnable and prove the feasibilty. see if you can use a model as example and form a full version of experiment version of the whole design and get a first version of result batch for me to see what the output can be and whether this direction is worth doing.
make the major innovation to be more focused on the pipeline building, focus on the output result but it can be trade-off a bit innovation level by the pipeline building.
build a full vversion of Github repo with code and can be rerun locally and shared through the github link
